title: [FIRRTL] Simplify internal Analog connections
body:
Context for this is in #4112. Resolves #4112.

For all analog connections which are only internal to a module, generate a wire for that connection, rather than the alias/assign construct.

### Questions

I'm not completely sure that I've implemented this properly. When running the tests that I added, I saw that the output from the tests looked like it had some extra statements that I didn't expect. Perhaps this is just due to me not being so familiar with the expected MLIR output. For example, for one test, the input looks like this:

```mlir
firrtl.module @AnalogMergeThree() {
  %result_iInA = firrtl.instance iInA @AnalogInModA(in a: !firrtl.analog<8>)
  %result_iInB = firrtl.instance iInB @AnalogInModB(in a: !firrtl.analog<8>)
  %result_iOut = firrtl.instance iOut @AnalogOutModA(out a: !firrtl.analog<8>)
  firrtl.attach %result_iInA, %result_iInB, %result_iOut : !firrtl.analog<8>, !firrtl.analog<8>, !firrtl.analog<8>
}
```

The output looks like this:

```mlir
hw.module @AnalogMergeThree() {
  %.a.wire = sv.wire : !hw.inout<i8>
  hw.instance "iInA" @AnalogInModA(a: %.a.wire: !hw.inout<i8>) -> ()
  %.a.wire_0 = sv.wire {name = ".a.wire"} : !hw.inout<i8>
  hw.instance "iInB" @AnalogInModB(a: %.a.wire: !hw.inout<i8>) -> () 
  %.a.wire_1 = sv.wire {name = ".a.wire"} : !hw.inout<i8>
  hw.instance "iOut" @AnalogOutModA(a: %.a.wire: !hw.inout<i8>) -> ()
  hw.output
}
```

Should `%.a.wire_0` and `%.a.wire_1` be generated? Are they a byproduct of me not pruning MLIR nodes properly?
